steps.title: Identify the vertex from the given form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x-6)^2 + 3$ is already in vertex form $y = a(x-h)^2 + k$. Reading directly, the vertex is $(h, k) = (6, 3)$.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex's coordinates in context
steps.content:
In this problem:
  • $x$ represents time in seconds
  • $f(x)$ represents height in inches
The vertex at $(6, 3)$ means at $x = 6$ seconds, the height is 3 inches. Since $a = \frac{1}{9} > 0$, the parabola opens upward, so this vertex is the minimum point of the function.

steps.title: Determine what the minimum represents
steps.content: The minimum height of the parabola is the $y$-coordinate of the vertex: 3 inches. This is the lowest point the toy car reaches on its trajectory. That matches choice A — A is correct.
